title: Common Cold
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: The common cold is a viral upper respiratory tract infection.
Antibiotics treat bacterial infections and do not treat viral colds.
Care is supportive and may include fluids, rest, symptom medicines, and careful label reading.
Patients should seek care for trouble breathing, worsening symptoms, or symptoms that improve and then return.
